isolation.tool: rg (ripgrep) text searches + Read tool for code review
isolation.scope: iOS source + shared Rust layer in /Users/kai/dev/projects/litter

assertions.title: Discovery subtitle shows detected agent types

steps.action: Search for agent type display in discovery row views
steps.expected: DiscoveryView server rows show detected agent types in subtitle or badge row
steps.observed: DiscoveryView.swift:492-498 shows detected agent types in a horizontal ScrollView with AgentBadgeView when count > 1 or first != .codex. The DiscoveredServer model (DiscoveredServer.swift:59) carries agentTypes field. DiscoveryView.swift:589-595 has discoveryAgentTypes(for:) function that reads server.agentTypes and syncs AgentSelectionStore.

steps.action: Verify DiscoveredServer model has agentTypes field
steps.expected: agentTypes property exists on DiscoveredServer with default [.codex]
steps.observed: DiscoveredServer.swift:59: `let agentTypes: [AgentType]`, default `[.codex]` at line 78. NetworkDiscovery.swift:295-296 populates agentTypes from Rust discovery data.

steps.expected: XCTest cases verify agent types on DiscoveredServer
steps.observed: ConnectionConversationParityTests.swift has testDiscoveredServerCarriesAgentTypes (3 types), testDiscoveredServerWithOnlyCodexHasSingleAgentType, testDiscoveredServerDefaultAgentTypesIsCodex

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/DiscoveryView.swift:492-498 — agent type badge row in server row

assertions.title: Server connection flow handles agent selection before connecting

steps.action: Search for agent picker presentation before connection
steps.expected: Agent picker shown before connection for multi-agent servers
steps.observed: DiscoveryView.swift:707-713 checks `needsPicker` when compatibleTypes.count > 1 and no prior selection. If picker needed, sets agentPickerServer and returns without connecting. After picker selection (line 173-182), connection proceeds with chosen type.

steps.expected: AgentPickerSheet view for connection flow
steps.observed: AgentPickerView.swift:392-534: AgentPickerSheet with agent infos, compatible/incompatible filtering, selection callback. Sheet presented via DiscoveryView.swift:133 .sheet(item: $agentPickerServer).

steps.expected: Tests for picker show/skip logic
steps.observed: ConnectionConversationParityTests.swift:64-82 — testMultiAgentServerRequiresPickerWhenNoSelection, testMultiAgentServerSkipsPickerWhenSelectionExists

assertions.title: SSH connect flow detects agents and shows picker when needed

steps.action: Search for SSH agent detection and picker presentation
steps.expected: SSH flow detects agents, pauses for picker if multiple found
steps.observed: DiscoveryView.swift:228-251: During SSH connect, checks for pendingAgentSelection from runtime detection. When detected, sets agentPickerDetectedAgents and agentPickerServer, then shows picker. After selection (line 180-186), reconnectWithSelectedAgent is called. Rust side: connect_remote_over_ssh_with_agent_type (mobile_client/mod.rs:733) probes for agents via validate_agent_binary_on_host.

steps.expected: reconnectWithSelectedAgent function exists
steps.observed: DiscoveryView.swift:638-641: reconnectWithSelectedAgent(server:agentType:) re-initiates SSH connection with selected agent type.

steps.expected: Tests for SSH capability and detection
steps.observed: ConnectionConversationParityTests.swift:84-98 — testDiscoveredServerWithSSHCapabilityCanConnectViaSSH, testDiscoveredServerWithoutSSHCannotConnectViaSSH

assertions.title: Connection choice dialog includes agent-aware labels

steps.action: Search for 'Use Codex' in connection choice dialog
steps.expected: No 'Use Codex' text found; dialog uses provider-agnostic labels
steps.observed: rg found zero matches for 'Use Codex' across all iOS source. Dialog button says 'Use Agent (port)' (DiscoveryView.swift:197). SSH option says 'Connect via SSH' (line 204).

steps.expected: Message uses 'agent' not 'Codex'
steps.observed: DiscoveryView.swift:1388-1395: connectionChoiceMessage uses 'agent' terminology: 'Use SSH to bootstrap an agent', 'Agent is available on ports', 'Choose an agent port'. No Codex references.

steps.expected: Mode is directAgent not directCodex with backward compat
steps.observed: DiscoveredServer.swift:25: PreferredConnectionMode.directAgent. Line 28-39: backward compat decoding 'directCodex' → 'directAgent'. Connection step kinds from Rust: detectingAgents, findingAgent, installingAgent, startingAgent (AppServerSnapshot+UI.swift:109-121).

steps.expected: Tests for agent-generic labels and backward compat
steps.observed: ConnectionConversationParityTests.swift:100-128 — testConnectionChoiceMessageIsAgentGeneric, testPreferredConnectionModeDirectAgentLabel, testPreferredConnectionModeBackwardCompatibility

assertions.title: Codex connection flow works end-to-end

steps.action: Code review: verify Codex connection path exists
steps.expected: Codex WebSocket/in-process connection path is functional
steps.observed: Rust: CodexProvider (provider/codex.rs) wraps AppServerClient, implements ProviderTransport. create_codex_provider (provider/mod.rs:483-487) constructs Box<dyn ProviderTransport>. connect_local and connect_remote paths both create sessions via CodexProvider. Factory routes Codex to standard bootstrap_codex_server + WebSocket path (factory.rs:71-73).

steps.expected: iOS can initiate Codex connections
steps.observed: DiscoveryView handles .local, .remote, .remoteURL, .sshThenRemote connection targets. Codex connections use standard ServerSession path through MobileClient. All 6 agent types have display names, icons, tint colors, persistent keys verified in tests.

evidence.network: N/A (blocked: requires running Codex app-server on port 8390 for end-to-end testing)

assertions.issues: Requires remote Codex app-server for end-to-end testing. Code review verified all implementation components are present: CodexProvider, create_codex_provider factory, standard bootstrap_codex_server + WebSocket path, and iOS integration via DiscoveryView connection targets.

assertions.title: Pi connection flow works end-to-end

steps.action: Code review: verify Pi connection path exists
steps.expected: Pi Native and Pi ACP connection paths are implemented
steps.observed: Rust: PiNativeTransport (provider/pi/transport.rs) handles JSONL framing over SSH channel for 'pi --mode rpc'. PiAcpTransport (provider/pi/acp_transport.rs) wraps AcpClient for 'npx pi-acp' standard ACP. Factory (factory.rs:66-67) routes PiNative → create_pi_native, PiAcp → create_pi_acp. E2E tests in provider/pi/e2e.rs cover full lifecycle (14 test functions).

steps.expected: Pi can use Native or ACP transport based on preference
steps.observed: provider/pi/preference.rs has select_pi_transport that routes based on PiTransportPreference (auto/native/acp). Detection module (provider/pi/detection.rs) probes for Pi agent availability.

evidence.network: N/A (blocked: requires remote Pi agent server for end-to-end testing)

assertions.issues: Requires remote Pi agent server for end-to-end testing. Code review verified all implementation components are present: PiNativeTransport, PiAcpTransport, factory routing, transport preference logic, and 14 E2E mock transport tests.

assertions.title: Droid connection flow works end-to-end

steps.action: Code review: verify Droid connection path exists
steps.expected: Droid Native and Droid ACP connection paths are implemented
steps.observed: Rust: DroidNativeTransport (provider/droid/transport.rs) handles JSONRPC streaming for 'droid exec --input-format stream-jsonrpc --output-format stream-jsonrpc'. DroidAcpTransport (provider/droid/acp_transport.rs) is DEPRECATED, replaced by PiAcpTransport for standard ACP path via 'droid exec --output-format acp'. Factory (factory.rs:68-69) routes DroidNative → create_droid_native, DroidAcp → create_droid_acp (now uses PiAcpTransport).

steps.action: Verify Droid ACP migration to standard ACP
steps.expected: Droid ACP uses standard ACP command, not proprietary stream-json
steps.observed: factory.rs:145: create_droid_acp uses standard ACP transport. Test droid_acp_migration_uses_standard_acp_command verifies command is 'droid exec --output-format acp'. Old DroidAcpTransport is #[deprecated] with note to use PiAcpTransport.

evidence.network: N/A (blocked: requires remote Droid agent server for end-to-end testing)

assertions.issues: Requires remote Droid agent server for end-to-end testing. Code review verified all implementation components are present: DroidNativeTransport, migrated DroidAcp to standard ACP via PiAcpTransport, factory routing, and migration tests confirming standard ACP command.

assertions.title: GenericAcp connection flow works end-to-end

steps.action: Code review: verify GenericAcp connection path exists
steps.expected: GenericAcp transport with configurable remote command is implemented
steps.observed: Rust: GenericAcpTransport (provider/acp/generic_transport.rs) wraps AcpClient with configurable remote command from ProviderConfig.remote_command. Factory (factory.rs:70, 180) routes GenericAcp → create_generic_acp, which reads config.remote_command and spawns over SSH. Returns error when no remote_command configured. ProviderConfig has remote_command: Option<String> field.

steps.expected: iOS has ACP profiles and custom command text field for GenericAcp
steps.observed: ACPProfilesView.swift manages ACP provider profiles. ACPProfile.swift:52 — profiles used by agent picker for GenericAcp connections. AgentPickerSheet includes ACP profiles. Tests: testAllAgentTypesHaveDisplayNames, testAllAgentTypesHavePersistentKeys, testAllAgentTypesHaveIcons verify GenericAcp has full badge support.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Models/ACPProfile.swift:52 — ACP profiles used by agent picker
apps/ios/Tests/LitterTests/ConnectionConversationParityTests.swift:156-195 — agent type badge tests including GenericAcp
evidence.network: N/A (blocked: requires remote ACP-compatible agent server for end-to-end testing)

assertions.issues: Requires remote ACP-compatible agent server for end-to-end testing. Code review verified all implementation components are present: GenericAcpTransport with configurable remote command, factory routing, iOS ACP profile management, and comprehensive test coverage for GenericAcp badge/icon/display properties.

blockers.description: VAL-IOS-035 through VAL-IOS-038 require live remote agent servers (Codex, Pi, Droid, GenericAcp) for end-to-end connection testing. These cannot be validated without network access to running agent instances.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: N/A — infrastructure limitation, not a transient failure

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-IOS-028 through VAL-IOS-031), 4 blocked (VAL-IOS-035 through VAL-IOS-038). All code-reviewed assertions show correct implementation: discovery rows display agent type badges, agent picker is shown before connecting to multi-agent servers, SSH flow detects agents and pauses for picker, connection choice dialog uses provider-agnostic labels with no 'Use Codex' text. Blocked assertions require live remote agent servers for end-to-end validation; code review confirmed all transport implementations exist in Rust (CodexProvider, PiNativeTransport, PiAcpTransport, DroidNativeTransport, GenericAcpTransport) with proper factory routing. 28 iOS XCTest cases in ConnectionConversationParityTests.swift cover the code-reviewed assertions.
